column4: Latin "tolerare" + productive -able suffix, standard EModE adjective formation
ordinary EModE noun (French/Latin loan, in use well before 1611)
transparent compound of two common nouns "town" + "clerk"
EModE spelling variant of "traffic" with productive -ick ending (cf. "musick", "publick")
Latin trans- + "parent" (appearing), attested EModE adjective formation
ordinary plural of attested Latinate noun "tutor"
possessive -'s on a bare cardinal numeral is an unusual construction not clearly grounded independently; safer to avoid the possessive form
transparent compound "two" + "edged" (past participle of attested verb "edge")
archaic strong past participle -en on attested verb "uphold" (cf. ME strong participles)
utter + productive superlative -ermost, same pattern as "uppermost"
spelling could be "veils" (ai/ei EModE variance) or the obscure obsolete noun "vails" (perquisites); ambiguous without independent attestation
superlative -est on adjective "valiant", productive though unusual for a polysyllabic adjective (cf. EModE "honestest")
-ed past tense of attested Latinate verb "violate"
ordinary attested EModE noun (war + agentive -or, well-established)
transparent compound "wine" + "bibber" (agentive -er on archaic verb "bib", to drink)
transparent compound "wine" + "fat" (archaic sense of "fat" = vat, attested)
-ed past tense of verbal use of "winter"
plural of attested noun "with/withe" (a flexible willow band, cf. Middle English "withe")
-edst 2nd-person past inflection ("thou woundedst"), standard EModE thou-form
standard past tense of attested verb "weave"
plural of the ordinarily uncountable abstract noun "wrath" is an unusual pluralization not clearly grounded independently
archaic strong past participle -en on "wreathe", cf. ME strong participle pattern
